messages.content:
What factors led to World War I?

What factors led to World War I?

Militarism, Alliances, Imperialism, Nationalism

Extremism, Treaties, Betrayal, Gunpowder

Aggression, Fascism, Guns, Nazis

none of these

These factors all played a role in leading to World War I, but the primary factors were militarism, alliances, imperialism, and nationalism. These factors created a tense and competitive atmosphere among the major powers of Europe, eventually leading to the outbreak of the war in 1914.

Who was in the Triple Entente?

Who was in the Triple Entente?

Great Britain

France

Russia

all of these
All of these countries were part of the Triple Entente during World War I.

What was the style of warfare used in WWI that included digging miles of hideouts by both sides and a space between their territories called 'no man's land?'

What was the style of warfare used in WWI that included digging miles of hideouts by both sides and a space between their territories called 'no man's land?'

world warfare

tunnel warfare

trench warfare

hideout warfare
The style of warfare used in World War I that included digging miles of hideouts by both sides and a space between their territories called 'no man's land' is called trench warfare.

What did U.S. President Woodrow Wilson propose as a framework for ending WWI?

Group of answer choices
Wilson's Treatise

The Fourteen Points

Point of Order

the Peace Accords
U.S. President Woodrow Wilson proposed the Fourteen Points as a framework for ending World War I.

What international body was proposed by Wilson and adopted in Europe to help prevent future wars?
Group of answer choices
The European Peace Club

The League of Extraordinary Gentlemen

The European Union

League of Nations
The League of Nations was proposed by Woodrow Wilson and adopted in Europe to help prevent future wars.

What event ignited World War I?

What event ignited World War I?

the assassination of Archduke Franz Fernidand

the kidnap of Woodrow Wilson's son

the publication of Mien Kampf

the bombing of Hiroshima
The event that ignited World War I was the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand of Austria-Hungary in Sarajevo, Bosnia on June 28, 1914.

Who led the Bolshevik revolution in Russia?

Who led the Bolshevik revolution in Russia?

Adolf Hitler

Tsar Nicholas II

Vladimir Lenin

Joseph Stalin
Vladimir Lenin led the Bolshevik revolution in Russia.

What political party did the Bolsheviks evolve into?

What political party did the Bolsheviks evolve into?

the Communist Party

the Socialist Party

the Nazi Party

the Counter-Revolutionaries
The Bolsheviks evolved into the Communist Party.

Who took over the Communist Party and leadership of the U.S.S.R. when Lenin died?

Who took over the Communist Party and leadership of the U.S.S.R. when Lenin died?

Adolf Hitler

Joseph Stalin

Woodrow Wilson

Winston Churchill
Joseph Stalin took over the Communist Party and leadership of the Soviet Union (U.S.S.R.) when Lenin died.
